agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Environmental Awards recipients to be presented by Mayor Hurley
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on recognizing recipients of the Environmental Awards, presented by Mayor Hurley. This is a ceremonial recognition, not a new regulation or bylaw. The decision will honor individuals or groups for environmental contributions in Burnaby. No new rules or costs for businesses are proposed.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.1 Environmental Awards Recipients
Presenter:
Mayor Hurley

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to consider task force economic growth report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will hear a presentation on the final report from the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. The report, prepared by Deloitte consultants, outlines strategies to boost the local economy. This is a proposed set of ideas and recommendations — no decisions have been made yet. Business owners should watch for future council votes on specific actions that could affect their operations.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for future council meetings where specific recommendations from this report may be voted on — your input could matter.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
6.1 INVITED PRESENTATION: Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Final Report
Presenter:
Amanda Fung, Manager Economic Strategy & Impact Strategy & Business Design, Deloitte; and Karin Hung, Director Strategic Initiatives

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Mayor's task force plan for economic growth
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on recommendations from the Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience. This is a proposed plan, not a final decision yet. The task force report includes ideas to help local businesses grow and make the economy stronger. Council will consider these recommendations at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the plan could lead to new programs or changes that affect how businesses operate in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your business operations or access to new support programs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1 FINAL REPORT - MAYOR'S TASK FORCE ON ECONOMIC GROWTH AND RESILIENCE
Purpose: To provide Council with recommendations regarding the Final Report of the Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on rezoning at 7211 Greenford Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a rezoning application for the property at 7211 Greenford Avenue. The proposal, called REZ #25-25, would change the official zoning rules for this land. Council will first vote on whether to give the application 'First and Second Reading' — this is a procedural step that allows the proposal to move forward for public consultation and further review. A final decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this rezoning could affect nearby property owners and developers.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2 REZ #25-25 - 7211 GREENFORD AVENUE
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-25 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Development Variance Permit for 8888 University Drive
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a portion of 8888 University Drive. A Development Variance Permit is a special permission that allows a property owner to slightly bend certain city zoning rules — for example, building setbacks or height limits — without having to go through a full rezoning process. This decision is not final yet; Council will consider and vote on the permit at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required — monitor the council decision to see if the permit is approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3 DVP #26-08 - PORTION OF 8888 UNIVERSITY DRIVE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-08.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: BC Hydro EV chargers at Burnaby Lake Sports Complex
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to let BC Hydro install and operate 10 Level 3 DC fast-charging EV stalls (converting 14 existing parking stalls) in the east parking lot at 3860 Sperling Avenue. BC Hydro would cover all design, construction, operation, maintenance, and electricity costs under a nominal licence agreement with an initial 10-year term and a 10-year renewal option. Charging fees would align with the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, and BC Hydro would keep the charging revenue.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. Watch for the council decision on June 23, 2026.

key_dates.label: Council vote on BC Hydro EV charger licence agreement

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4 BC HYDRO PUBLIC EV CHARGING PROGRAM - BURNABY LAKE SPORTS COMPLEX EAST PARKING LOT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to enter into a nominal license agreement to allow BC Hydro to install, operate, and maintain public electric vehicle charging infrastructure within the Burnaby Lake Sports Complex - East parking lot

attachments.title: BC Hydro Public EV Charging Program - Burnaby Lake Sports Complex East Parking L

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on HVAC maintenance contract for city buildings
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award to Broadway Refrigeration and Air Conditioning for HVAC (heating, ventilation, and air conditioning) maintenance and repair services. If approved, this contract would cover all City-owned buildings and facilities. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming Council meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5 CA - HVAC SYSTEMS MAINTENANCE AND REPAIR SERVICES - BROADWAY REFRIGERATION AND AIR CONDITIONING
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for HVAC systems maintenance and repair services of City owned buildings and facilities

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on HVAC maintenance contract for city buildings
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for HVAC (heating, ventilation, and air conditioning) maintenance and repair services for all city-owned buildings and facilities. The recommended contractor is Just Mechanical. If approved, this contract would cover routine upkeep and emergency repairs to keep city buildings comfortable and safe. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6 CA - HVAC SYSTEMS MAINTENANCE AND REPAIR SERVICES - JUST MECHANICAL
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for HVAC systems maintenance and repair services of City owned buildings and facilities.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on Squint Lake Park washroom construction contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a construction contract for a new washroom building at Squint Lake Park. This is a proposed decision — the contract has not been approved yet. If approved, the project would move forward with a chosen contractor to build the washroom facility. The specific contractor and contract amount are detailed in the full agenda report.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7 CA - SQUINT LAKE PARK WASHROOM BUILDING - CONSTRUCTION
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Squint Lake Park Washroom Building – Construction.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for external audit services
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for external audit services. This is a routine financial oversight decision — an external audit is an independent check of the city's financial records to make sure they are accurate and follow the rules. If approved, the chosen firm will audit the city's books for the upcoming fiscal year. This does not directly change any business rules or fees, but it ensures the city's finances are properly managed.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8 CA - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the External Audit Services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on contract for Burnaby Lake aquatics timing system
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for the design, supply, and installation of a new timing system for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex pool. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet. If approved, the contract would go to a company to build and install the system that tracks swim times and race results. The total cost and the chosen company will be decided at the council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time — this is an internal city contract decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9 CA - DESIGN, SUPPLY, AND INSTALL AQUATICS TIMING SYSTEM
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Design, Supply, and Installation of an aquatics timing system for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for Vancouver to SFU cycling connection
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract change for the Vancouver to SFU Cycling Connection project. This is a proposed change to an existing contract for building a bike route between Vancouver and Simon Fraser University. The decision has not been made yet — Council is set to vote on this at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.10 CC - (EA07030) VANCOUVER TO SFU CYCLING CONNECTION
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) to the (EA7030) Vancouver to SFU Cycling Connection project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on contract change for crosswalk improvements at 5 sites
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing construction contract) for the Crosswalk Improvements – 5 Sites project. If approved, this change would adjust the scope or cost of the work being done to improve pedestrian crossings at five locations across the city. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.11 CC - (CA - 7015) CROSSWALK IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) to the (CA-7015) Crosswalk Improvements – 5 Sites project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing sand and gravel supply contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing the city's contract for the supply and delivery of sand and gravel. This is a proposed contract renewal that would extend the existing agreement. If approved, the renewal would ensure a continued supply of these materials for city projects. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on this item at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.12 CR - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY OF SAND AND GRAVEL
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Sand and Gravel.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing Centennial Foods supply contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed contract renewal for the supply and delivery of food products from Centennial Foods Vancouver. If approved, this would extend the existing agreement for continued food product deliveries. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming Council meeting. Business owners who rely on these food supplies should monitor the outcome, as a change could affect their supply chain.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — a renewal approval could affect your food supply arrangements.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.13 CR - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY OF FOOD PRODUCTS (CENTENNIAL FOODS VANCOUVER)
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for the supply and delivery of food products.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing food supply contract with GFS British Columbia
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with GFS British Columbia for the supply and delivery of food products. If approved, this contract renewal would allow the city to continue buying food from this supplier for its operations. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — this is an internal city procurement decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.14 CR - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY OF FOOD PRODUCTS (GFS BRITISH COLUMBIA)
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for the supply and delivery of food products.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Sysco food supply contract renewal
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing the city's contract with Sysco Vancouver for the supply and delivery of food products. This proposed contract renewal would allow the city to continue buying food from Sysco for its operations, such as for community centres, senior centres, and other city-run facilities. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on this at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.15 CR - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY OF FOOD PRODUCTS (SYSCO VANCOUVER)
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for the supply and delivery of food products.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider supporting a nationwide Chinese Canadian Heritage Month
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a motion to support the creation of a nationwide Chinese Canadian Heritage Month. This is a proposal to formally back a federal initiative that would recognize the contributions and history of Chinese Canadians. If approved, the City of Burnaby would send a letter of support to the federal government. The decision does not create any new local rules or fees for businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1 CHC - NOM: CHINESE CANADIAN HERITAGE MONTH
Purpose: To seek support for a nation wide Chinese Canadian Heritage Month.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on 2025/2026 Community School Grant payments
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to approve the 2025/2026 Community School Grant payment. This grant provides funding to local community schools for programs and services. If approved, the money will be issued to support these schools in the upcoming school year. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider this at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2 ECC - COMMUNITY SCHOOL GRANT FUNDING 2025/2026
Purpose: To obtain Council approval to issue the 2025/2026 Community School Grant payment.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider 2026 General Local Election preparations
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to review a report on preparations and timelines for the 2026 General Local Election, which also includes the election for School District No. 41. This is a proposed overview of the election process, not a final decision. The report outlines key dates and steps for the upcoming municipal election, but no rules or bylaws have been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1 2026 GENERAL LOCAL ELECTION
Purpose: To provide an overview of the 2026 General Local Election preparations and timelines, which also includes the School District No. 41 election.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to review 2025 Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation work
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider a report on the work of the Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation Division in 2025. This is an update on the division's activities and progress over the past year. No new rules or requirements are proposed at this time — the report is for information only. Council is not voting on any new bylaws or policies as part of this agenda item.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2 2025 INDIGENOUS RELATIONS AND RECONCILIATION DIVISION - A YEAR IN REVIEW
Purpose: To update Council on the work of the Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation Division in 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to receive 2025 Statement of Financial Information
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the 2025 Statement of Financial Information (SOFI), a legally required annual report that details the city's revenues, expenses, and financial position. This is a routine presentation for information only — no immediate changes to business fees, taxes, or regulations are proposed. The report is being presented to meet the statutory requirement under BC's Community Charter.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3 2025 STATEMENT OF FINANCIAL INFORMATION (SOFI)
Purpose: To present the 2025 Statement of Financial Information in accordance with the statutory requirement.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby heritage program updates could affect property owners
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider updates to the City's heritage program initiatives. This is a proposed change that has not yet been approved. The update may introduce new rules or incentives for owners of heritage-designated properties, including possible requirements for maintenance, alterations, or applications for heritage grants. Business owners with older or historically significant buildings should pay attention, as these changes could affect property use, renovation plans, or eligibility for financial help. The exact details of the proposed changes are not yet public, but council is set to vote on the update at a future meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and review the proposed heritage program changes once published — they may affect your property or renovation plans.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4 CHC - HERITAGE PROGRAM INITIATIVES
Purpose: To provide an update on heritage program initiatives.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to review amenity and development cost charge programs
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider an annual report on the Amenity Cost Charge (ACC) and Development Cost Charge (DCC) programs as of December 31, 2025. These charges are fees the city collects from new development projects to help pay for community amenities (like parks, community centres, and libraries) and infrastructure (like roads, water, and sewer systems). The report provides an update on how much money has been collected and spent under these programs. Council will use this information to decide if any changes are needed to the charges or how the funds are used. This is a proposed review — no changes have been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — changes to these charges could affect the cost of future development projects in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5 FMC - ACC AND DCC ANNUAL REPORT (2025) - 2026-06-23
Purpose: To provide information regarding the Amenity and Development Cost Charge Programs as of December 31, 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to use hotel tax funds for affordable housing
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to use money collected from a special tax on online accommodation platforms (like Airbnb and VRBO) to support affordable housing in Burnaby. This tax is called the Municipal and Regional District Tax (MRDT), and it is charged to guests who book short-term rentals. The proposal would direct a portion of these funds away from general tourism promotion and into housing projects. A final decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how short-term rental taxes are spent in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6 FMC - USE OF ONLINE ACCOMMODATION PLATFORM (OAP) MRDT FUNDS FOR AFFORDABLE HOUSING
Purpose: To inform Council of the total collection and allocation of Online Accommodation Platform (OAP) Municipal and Regional District Tax (MRDT) revenues to support affordable housing in Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to review status of major civic projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on significant civic projects in Burnaby for June 2026. This is a proposed review of ongoing projects — no final decisions have been made yet. The update will inform council about progress, timelines, and any changes to major city-building initiatives. Business owners should watch for potential impacts on construction, development, and local infrastructure that could affect their operations.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor council's decision — project updates may affect construction timelines, permits, or business access in your area.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.7 FMC – SU – SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS JUNE 2026
Purpose: To provide a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive Building Permit Activity Report for May 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from May 1 to May 31, 2026. This report is for information only — it summarizes construction activity in the city during that period. No bylaw changes or new rules are being proposed. The report helps Council track how much development is happening in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.8 SU:  BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 05 FROM MAY 1 - MAY 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Two high-rise towers (32 & 28 storeys) at 6470 & 6508 Silver Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a zoning bylaw amendment that would allow two high-rise apartment buildings — 32 and 28 storeys — at 6470 and 6508 Silver Avenue. The proposal includes about 784 total units, with 83 of those set aside as rental replacement units for existing tenants. This new plan replaces an earlier proposal for a 33-storey strata building and a 6-storey rental building. Council will consider this at an upcoming meeting; it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby Council before the vote to share your support or concerns.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1 #14832 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 11, 2026 - REZ #25-12 (6470 & 6508 Silver Avenue)
Purpose: to permit two high-rise apartment buildings of 32 and 28 storeys respectively, with a total of approximately 784 units, inclusive of 83 rental replacement units. The subject application supersedes REZ #21-36, which proposed a 33-storey strata apartment and a 6-storey rental replacement building. The new proposal is consistent with the Burnaby 2050 OCP land use designation for the site.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 45- and 35-storey towers with 1,065 units on Dow Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a zoning amendment that would allow a large mixed-use development at 6645-6707 Dow Avenue. The proposal includes two high-rise apartment towers (45 and 35 storeys) with shops on Beresford Street, plus a 6-storey apartment building. A total of about 1,065 homes are planned, including 112 rental units that must replace existing rentals. This new plan replaces an earlier proposal for a 51-storey tower. The decision has not been made yet — Council will give the bylaw its first two readings before a public hearing.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed towers and rental units.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2 #14833 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 12, 2026 - REZ #25-23 (6645/59/75/91 and 6707 Dow Avenue)
Purpose: to permit a mixed-use high-density development comprising of two high-rise apartment buildings of 45 and 35 storeys respectively, with commercial fronting Beresford Street, as well as a 6-storey apartment building. Approximately 1,065 dwelling units are proposed, inclusive of 112 required rental replacement units. The subject application supersedes REZ #18-49, which proposed a 51-storey mixed-use residential and commercial tower, a 41-storey apartment tower, and a 6-storey rental building. The new proposal is consistent with the Burnaby 2050 OCP land use designations for the site.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Repeal of housing agreement bylaw for 5227 Lane Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would repeal the existing Housing Agreement Bylaw for the property at 5227 Lane Street. A housing agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that sets rules about how the property can be used — often to keep it affordable or for specific housing types. If approved, this repeal would remove those restrictions from that property. The decision has not been made yet; this is an upcoming item on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1 #14836 - Burnaby Housing Agreement (5227 Lane Street) Bylaw 2024, Repeal Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to repeal Burnaby Housing Agreement (5227 Lane Street) Bylaw 2024

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on zoning change for 6800 Lougheed Highway development
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed zoning bylaw amendment that would allow a large mixed-use development at 6800 Lougheed Highway, as part of Phase 1A of the Burnaby Lake Village Master Plan. The plan would permit construction of three mid-rise apartment buildings (16, 17, and 19 storeys) with ground-floor shops and restaurants, all built above an underground parking garage. This is a proposed change — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby Council before the vote to share your support or concerns.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.1 #14781 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 18, 2025 - REZ #25-10 (Ptn. of 6800 Lougheed Highway)
Purpose: to advance Phase 1A of the Burnaby Lake Village Master Plan to permit the construction of a mixed-use residential development with ground-oriented neighbourhood commercial, above an underground parkade, comprised of three mid-rise residential apartment buildings G1, G2 and H with heights of 17, 19 and 16 storeys respectively, inclusive of 2 storeys of neighbourhood commercial at the base of G1 and G2

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026 up for final adoption
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would set new rules for how developers must provide and pay for essential services like roads, water, sewer, and drainage when they build on land in Burnaby. If approved, it will replace the current bylaw and update requirements for development-related works and services. The bylaw aims to make sure new developments properly connect to and support the city's infrastructure.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if adopted, this bylaw will affect all future development applications in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.2 #14829 - Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to respect provision of works and services in respect to development of land

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Works and Services Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a proposed bylaw that would create a new 'Works and Services Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund'. This fund would hold money paid by developers instead of building certain public works (like roads, sidewalks, or water lines) as part of their projects. If approved, the city would use this reserve fund to pay for those public works itself. The decision has not been made yet — this is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.3 #14830 - Burnaby Works and Services Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish a Works and Services Payment-In-Lieu Reserve Fund

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby fee changes for works & services up for final vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of an amendment to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would change fees for works and services — for example, permits, inspections, or other city services related to construction, development, or property maintenance. If approved, the new fees would take effect after the vote. Business owners who pay city fees for building, plumbing, or similar services should watch for the outcome.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new fees for works and services will apply to your permits or city service requests.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.4 #14831 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 5, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (works & services)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026 up for final adoption
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on the final adoption of the new Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would replace the current sign rules and regulate all signs in the city, including business signs, billboards, and temporary signs. If approved, the bylaw will set new rules for sign size, placement, lighting, and permits. The goal is to update and clarify the rules for signs across Burnaby. Council will consider this at an upcoming meeting — it has not been passed yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if adopted, review the new rules to ensure your business signs comply.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.5 #14834 - Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to regulate signs in Burnaby

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby sign enforcement bylaw amendment up for final vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, specifically regarding signs. If approved, this bylaw amendment would change how sign-related violations are enforced and ticketed in the city. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider final adoption at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this could change how sign violations are enforced for your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.6 #14835 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (signs)

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Mayor Hurley to present Burnaby Environmental Awards recipients
minutes_briefs.summary: This item was on the council agenda for information only. The Mayor was scheduled to present the recipients of the Burnaby Environmental Awards. Because this was a ceremonial presentation and not a decision item, no vote was taken. Council simply received the information and no further action was required.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
3.1 Environmental Awards Recipients
Presenter:
Mayor Hurley

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received Mayor's Task Force economic growth report
minutes_briefs.summary: This item was listed on the agenda as an invited presentation of the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Final Report. The action verb was 'RECEIVE', which means council was only expected to hear the information, not to vote on it. Since no vote was required, there was no decision to make — the item was for information only.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
6.1 INVITED PRESENTATION: Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Final Report
Presenter:
Amanda Fung, Manager Economic Strategy & Impact Strategy & Business Design, Deloitte; and Karin Hung, Director Strategic Initiatives

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Mayor's Task Force economic growth recommendations
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously endorsed the recommendations from the Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience. Council voted to have staff study the feasibility and costs of these recommendations and report back. Council also authorized staff to create a new Economic Development Strategy based on the Task Force report. This strategy will guide the city's future economic direction. A copy of the report will be sent to each Task Force member.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required. Watch for future reports on the Economic Development Strategy that may affect your business.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT the recommendations of the Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience (Attachment 1) in the report titled "Final Report — Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth 'and Resilience" dated June 23, 2026, be endorsed; THAT staff be directed to review the feasibility, resource requirements, and implementation considerations associated with the Task Force recommendations, and to report back to Council; THAT Council authorize staff to prepare an Economic Development Strategy, informed by the Task Force report, as a strategic framework for future direction; and THAT a copy of this report be provided to each member of the Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for mixed-use development at 7211 Greenford Avenue
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to advance a rezoning bylaw for a high-density mixed-use development at 7211 Greenford Avenue. The decision authorizes the preparation of a rezoning bylaw (REZ #25-25) and directs it to first and second reading at a future council meeting. Council also approved a Housing Agreement (a legal contract that ensures affordable housing units are included in the project) and authorized the City Solicitor to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw. Additionally, Council approved a density bonus payment (extra money paid by the developer in exchange for permission to build more floor space than normally allowed) plus related interest. Before the rezoning can be finally adopted, the developer must meet several conditions listed in Attachment 3 of the report.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required for business owners — the rezoning is still in early stages; watch for future public hearing notices if you operate near this site.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-25 be prepared and advanced to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting; THAT a Housing Agreement be authorized according to the terms outlined in Section 3.5 of the report titled "REZ #25-25 - 7211 Greenford Avenue - Proposed Amended High-Density Mixed-Use Development (Edmonds Town Centre Plan)", dated June 23, 2026, and the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw; THAT Council approve the payment of density bonus and related interest according to the terms outlined in Section 3.4 of the report; and THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report be established as prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-25. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved fence height variances at 8888 University Drive
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a portion of 8888 University Drive. This permit allows the property owner to build fences that are taller than what the current zoning bylaw normally allows. A Development Variance Permit is a special permission that lets a property owner deviate from certain city rules. Council also directed the City Solicitor to register this permit with the Land Title Office, which makes the decision official on the property's legal record.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own property near 8888 University Drive, check with the City of Burnaby planning department to see if this variance affects your property's development rights.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT the issuance of DVP #26-08 included as Attachment 2 in the report titled "DVP #26-08 – Portion of 8888 University Drive – Fence Height Variances" dated June 23, 2026, be approved; and THAT the City Solicitor be directed to register notice of DVP #26-08 with the Land Title Office. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved BC Hydro EV charging at Burnaby Lake Sports Complex
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a nominal license agreement with BC Hydro to install, operate, and maintain public electric vehicle charging stations in the Burnaby Lake Sports Complex – East parking lot. A nominal license agreement means BC Hydro pays a small, symbolic fee for the right to use the land. This decision is final and the charging infrastructure will be installed as outlined in the June 23, 2026 report.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT the City enter into a nominal license agreement with British Columbia Hydro and Power Authority (BC Hydro) to permit BC Hydro to install, operate, and maintain public electric vehicle charging infrastructure within the Burnaby Lake Sports Complex – East parking lot, as outlined in the report titled "BC Hydro Public EV Charging Program – Burnaby Lake Sports Complex East Parking Lot" dated June 23, 2026. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $696,341 HVAC maintenance contract to Broadway Refrigeration
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved awarding a contract to Broadway Refrigeration and Air Conditioning for HVAC systems maintenance and repair services. The contract has an estimated cost of $696,341 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and the hourly rates submitted by the company.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract award to Broadway Refrigeration and Air Conditioning for an estimated cost of $696,341 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CA – HVAC Systems Maintenance and Repair Services – Broadway Refrigeration and Air Conditioning" dated June 23, 2026, be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and hourly rates submitted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $1.75M HVAC maintenance contract with Just Mechanical
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a contract award to Just Mechanical Ltd. for HVAC systems maintenance and repair services. The contract has an estimated cost of $1,747,650 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and the hourly rates submitted. This decision is final and the contract is now in effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract award to Just Mechanical Ltd. for an estimated cost of $1,747,650 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CA – HVAC Systems Maintenance and Repair Services – Just Mechanical Ltd." dated June 23, 2026, be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and hourly rates submitted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Squint Lake Park washroom construction for $1,168,303
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved awarding a construction contract to Alfred Horie Construction Co. Ltd. for $1,168,303 (including taxes) to build a new washroom building at Squint Lake Park. Council also approved a contingency fund of $116,831 (including tax) to cover any unexpected costs. The final payment will be based on the actual amount of work and materials delivered, as per the prices in the original bid. This decision is final and the project will move forward.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract award to Alfred Horie Construction Co. Ltd. for an estimated amount of $1,168,303 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CA – Squint Lake Park Washroom Building - Construction" dated June 23, 2026, be approved; THAT a contingency for an estimated amount of $116,831, including tax be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantities of goods and services delivered and prices as tendered. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved KPMG external audit contract for $741,333
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved awarding a contract to KPMG LLP for external audit services. The contract is for an estimated amount of $741,333, including taxes. The final payment will be based on the actual quantities of goods and services delivered and the unit prices proposed. This decision was carried unanimously by Council.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract award to KPMG LLP for an estimated amount of $741,333 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CA – External Audit Services" dated June 23, 2026 be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantities of goods and services delivered and unit prices as proposed. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $861,909 contract for aquatics timing system
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a contract award to Robert R Traynor (a sole proprietorship, meaning a business owned by one person) for the design, supply, and installation of an aquatics timing system. The total estimated cost is $861,909 including taxes. Council also approved a contingency (extra money set aside for unexpected costs) of $43,100 including taxes. The final payment will be based on the actual amount of goods and services delivered and the hourly rates submitted. The decision was carried unanimously, meaning all council members voted in favour.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract award to Robert R Traynor (sole proprietorship) for an estimated cost of $861,909 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CA – Design, Supply and Install Aquatics Timing System" dated June 23, 2026, be approved; THAT a contingency for an estimated amount of $43,100 including taxes, be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and hourly rates submitted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $292,367 contract change for Vancouver to SFU cycling connection
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a contract change to ISL Engineering and Land Services Ltd. for an estimated cost of $292,367 (including taxes) for the Vancouver to SFU Cycling Connection project. The final payment will be based on actual goods and services delivered and hourly rates submitted. This decision is now complete and the contract change is in effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract change to ISL Engineering and Land Services Ltd. for an estimated cost of $292,367 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CC – (EA-7030) Vancouver to SFU Cycling Connection" dated June 23, 2026 be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and hourly rates submitted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $338,076 contract change for crosswalk improvements to Crown Contracting
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a contract change to Crown Contracting Limited for an estimated $338,076 (including taxes) for crosswalk improvements. The final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and the hourly rates submitted. This decision was carried unanimously at the council meeting.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract change to Crown Contracting Limited for an estimated cost of $338,076 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CC – (CA-7015) Crosswalk Improvements" dated June 23, 2026; and THAT final payment be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and hourly rates submitted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $873,810 sand and gravel supply contract renewal with Utility West
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a contract renewal with Utility West Civil Works for the supply and delivery of sand and gravel. The estimated total cost is $873,810 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the actual quantity ordered and the unit prices quoted in the report. This decision is final and the contract is now in effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract renewal to Utility West Civil Works, for an estimated total cost of $873,810 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CR – Supply and Delivery of Sand and Gravel" dated June 23, 2026 be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity ordered and unit prices quoted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $700,000 food supply contract renewal with Centennial Foods Vancouver
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a contract renewal with Centennial Foods Vancouver for the supply and delivery of food products. The contract is valued at an estimated $700,000 including taxes. Final payment will be based on actual quantities ordered and the unit prices quoted in the agreement. This decision was made at the council meeting and is now final.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract renewal to Centennial Foods Vancouver, for an estimated cost of $700,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CR – Supply and Delivery of Food Products (Centennial Foods Vancouver)" dated June 23, 2026, be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on actual quantities ordered and unit prices quoted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a contract renewal with GFS British Columbia for the supply and delivery of food products. The contract is valued at an estimated $700,000 including taxes. The final payment will be based on actual quantities ordered and the unit prices quoted in the agreement. This decision was carried unanimously by Council.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract renewal to GFS British Columbia, for an estimated cost of $700,000 including taxes,  as outlined in the report titled "CR – Supply and Delivery of Food Products (GFS British Columbia)" dated June 23, 2026, be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on actual quantities ordered and unit prices quoted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $1.2M food supply contract renewal with Sysco Vancouver
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a contract renewal with Sysco Vancouver for the supply and delivery of food products. The contract is valued at an estimated $1,200,000 including taxes. Final payment will be based on actual quantities ordered and the unit prices quoted in the agreement. This decision was carried unanimously by Council.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT a contract renewal to Sysco Vancouver, for an estimated cost of $1,200,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CR – Supply and Delivery of Food Products (Sysco Vancouver)" dated June 23, 2026, be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on actual quantities ordered and unit prices quoted. minutes_briefs.title: Council unanimously approved supporting a nationwide Chinese Canadian Heritage Month
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to support the Government of Canada in formally proclaiming a nationwide Chinese Canadian Heritage Month. Council also committed to recognizing and celebrating Chinese Heritage Month annually in Burnaby and to supporting initiatives that promote cultural understanding, inclusion, and community engagement. The motion will be forwarded to the Minister of Canadian Heritage, all Burnaby Members of Parliament, the Federation of Canadian Municipalities, the Union of Municipalities of British Columbia, and large urban municipalities across Canada.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Council support the Government of Canada to formally proclaim a Chinese Canadian Heritage Month nationwide; and THAT Council commit to recognizing and celebrating Chinese Heritage Month annually and to supporting initiatives that promote cultural understanding, inclusion, and community engagement across Burnaby; and THAT Council forward this motion to the Minister of Canadian Heritage, all Burnaby Members of Parliament, the Federation of Canadian Municipalities and the Union of Municipalities of British Columbia, as well as to large urban municipalities across Canada. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $618,970 Community School Grant for 2025/2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a $618,970 Community School Grant payment for the 2025/2026 school year. This grant supports community school programs that provide after-school activities, childcare, and other services for local families. The funding was approved as outlined in a report dated June 2, 2026.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT the payment of the 2025/2026 Community School Grant, in the amount of $618,970, as outlined in the report titled "Community School Grant Funding 2025/2026" dated June 2, 2026, of the Executive Committee of Council meeting, be approved. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received 2026 General Local Election update
minutes_briefs.summary: Council received an overview of preparations and timelines for the 2026 General Local Election, which also includes the School District No. 41 election. No decision was made because this item was presented for information only — council was not asked to vote on it.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.1 2026 GENERAL LOCAL ELECTION
Purpose: To provide an overview of the 2026 General Local Election preparations and timelines, which also includes the School District No. 41 election.

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council reviewed 2025 Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation work
minutes_briefs.summary: Council was presented with a year-in-review update on the work of the Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation Division for 2025. The purpose was to inform Council, not to make a decision or vote. Because the item was for information only, no vote was taken or needed.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.2 2025 INDIGENOUS RELATIONS AND RECONCILIATION DIVISION - A YEAR IN REVIEW
Purpose: To update Council on the work of the Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation Division in 2025.

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received 2025 Statement of Financial Information
minutes_briefs.summary: This item was on the agenda for Burnaby Council to receive the 2025 Statement of Financial Information (SOFI), which is a legally required annual report on the city's finances. Because the action verb was 'RECEIVE' — meaning council was only being informed, not asked to vote — no decision was needed or made. The item was presented for information only, so there was no vote and no decision to report.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.3 2025 STATEMENT OF FINANCIAL INFORMATION (SOFI)
Purpose: To present the 2025 Statement of Financial Information in accordance with the statutory requirement.

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received update on Burnaby heritage program initiatives
minutes_briefs.summary: Council received an update on Burnaby's heritage program initiatives. Because the agenda item was for information only (the action verb was 'RECEIVE'), no vote was required and no decision was made. The item simply provided council with an update on heritage program initiatives that could affect property owners.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.4 CHC - HERITAGE PROGRAM INITIATIVES
Purpose: To provide an update on heritage program initiatives.

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Burnaby council reviewed amenity and development cost charge programs
minutes_briefs.summary: This item was on the agenda for information only. The purpose was to provide council with an update on the Amenity and Development Cost Charge Programs as of December 31, 2025. Because the action verb was 'RECEIVE' (to receive information), no vote was required or taken. Council simply reviewed the report and no decision was made.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.5 FMC - ACC AND DCC ANNUAL REPORT (2025) - 2026-06-23
Purpose: To provide information regarding the Amenity and Development Cost Charge Programs as of December 31, 2025.

minutes_briefs.title: [NOT REACHED] Council did not vote on using hotel tax funds for Burnaby affordable housing
minutes_briefs.summary: This item was on the agenda for Burnaby Council to consider using Online Accommodation Platform (OAP) Municipal and Regional District Tax (MRDT) revenues to support affordable housing. The purpose was to inform Council of the total collection and allocation of these funds. However, Council did not vote on this item because it was not reached during the meeting, likely due to time constraints.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.6 FMC - USE OF ONLINE ACCOMMODATION PLATFORM (OAP) MRDT FUNDS FOR AFFORDABLE HOUSING
Purpose: To inform Council of the total collection and allocation of Online Accommodation Platform (OAP) Municipal and Regional District Tax (MRDT) revenues to support affordable housing in Burnaby.

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received status update on major civic projects
minutes_briefs.summary: Council was scheduled to receive a status update on significant civic projects in Burnaby. The agenda item was for information only, meaning council was not expected to vote on it. No decision was made because the item was purely informational — council simply reviewed the update without taking any action.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.7 FMC – SU – SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS JUNE 2026
Purpose: To provide a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received Building Permit Activity Report for May 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Council was scheduled to receive the Building Permit Activity Report for May 2026, which summarizes construction activity based on permits issued during that period. Because the agenda item was classified as 'receive' (information only), no vote or decision was required or taken. Council simply noted the report without taking any action.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.8 SU:  BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 05 FROM MAY 1 - MAY 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved zoning amendment for 6470 & 6508 Silver Avenue
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give first and second readings to Bylaw No. 14832, which amends the zoning for properties at 6470 and 6508 Silver Avenue. This means the bylaw has passed two early stages of approval and will move forward to a public hearing before a final vote. The zoning change will allow different types of development on these properties than what was previously permitted.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Bylaw No. 14832 be now read a first and second time. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved first two readings of Dow Avenue zoning amendment
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give first and second reading to Bylaw No. 14833, which amends the zoning for properties at 6645-6707 Dow Avenue. This means the bylaw has passed two early stages of approval and will move forward to a public hearing before a final vote. The amendment changes the zoning rules for these specific properties, which could affect future development or land use on that site.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Bylaw No. 14833 be now read a first and second time. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved repeal of housing agreement bylaw for 5227 Lane Street
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to repeal a housing agreement bylaw for the property at 5227 Lane Street. The bylaw, Bylaw No. 14836, was given first, second, and third reading and passed. This means the previous housing agreement that was attached to this property is now cancelled. A housing agreement is a legal contract that usually requires a property to be used for affordable or special-needs housing. With the repeal, the property owner is no longer bound by those requirements.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required — the repeal is complete and in effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Bylaw No. 14836 be now read a first, second and third time. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved zoning amendment for 6800 Lougheed Highway
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to adopt Bylaw No. 14781, which changes the zoning rules for a portion of 6800 Lougheed Highway. This means the property now has a new official zoning designation, allowing different types of development or land use than before. The bylaw has been read a final time and is now law.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check with Burnaby's planning department to see how the new zoning affects your property or business plans at 6800 Lougheed Highway.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Bylaw No. 14781 be now read a final time and adopted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has unanimously passed the new Works and Services Bylaw 2026 (Bylaw No. 14829). This bylaw sets the rules and fees for construction, development, and other work that connects to the city's water, sewer, and drainage systems. It also covers road work permits and other public works services. The bylaw was given final approval at the council meeting and is now in effect.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new Works and Services Bylaw 2026 to understand updated fees and requirements before starting any new construction or development project in Burnaby.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Bylaw No. 14829 be now read a final time and adopted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Works and Services Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund Bylaw 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously passed Bylaw No. 14830, which creates a reserve fund for payments developers make instead of building certain public works and services. This bylaw is now final and in effect. The fund will hold money from developers who choose to pay cash instead of constructing things like roads, sewers, or parks. Council can then use this money to build those projects itself.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Bylaw No. 14830 be now read a final time and adopted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved final adoption of Burnaby fees and charges bylaw
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give final adoption to Bylaw No. 14831, which amends the city's Consolidated Fees and Charges bylaw. This means the updated fees and charges are now officially in effect. The bylaw sets the amounts the city charges for various permits, licences, and services.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check the new Consolidated Fees and Charges bylaw to see if any fees affecting your business have changed.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Bylaw No. 14831 be now read a final time and adopted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026 — final vote passed
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give final adoption to the new Sign Bylaw (Bylaw No. 14834). This means the bylaw is now law and in effect. The bylaw sets new rules for all signs on commercial and residential properties in Burnaby, including size limits, placement rules, and permit requirements. Business owners must follow these new rules for any new signs or changes to existing signs.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026 rules to ensure your existing and planned signs comply with the updated size, placement, and permit requirements.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Bylaw No. 14834 be now read a final time and adopted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Bylaw Notice Enforcement amendment bylaw
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give final adoption to Bylaw No. 14835, which amends the city's Bylaw Notice Enforcement system. This bylaw updates how certain municipal rules are enforced and how fines or penalties are handled. The change is now in effect following Council's final approval.
minutes_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT Bylaw No. 14835 be now read a final time and adopted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved wording for official opening plaques on six Burnaby projects
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved the wording for official opening plaques for six civic and engineering projects: Fire Station 4, Fire Station 8, Rosemary Brown Recreation Centre, Rowan Avenue Child Care Centre, Christine Sinclair Community Centre, and the Burnaby Lake Overpass. Council also voted to remove Section K from the Content and Design for Council Official Opening Plaques Policy, which means lead design and construction firms will no longer be named on the plaques. Staff have been authorized to fabricate and install the plaques.
minutes_briefs.source_body: THAT the proposed wording for Council Official Opening Plaques be approved for the following civic and engineering projects: • Fire Station 4; • Fire Station 8; • Rosemary Brown Recreation Centre; • Rowan Avenue Child Care Centre; • Christine Sinclair Community Centre; and • Burnaby Lake Overpass;   THAT Council not include section K of the Content and Design for Council Official Opening Plaques Policy, and remove lead design and construction firms; and THAT staff be authorized to proceed with the fabrication and installation of the plaques referenced in this report. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

_skipped.headline: Open Council Meeting Agenda for June 23, 2026
